Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERNEST LUBBE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2020 (no earlier date available). The following issues must be addressed: 1) Paragraph 1 of the petition sets forth an incorrect date of appointment. 2) Paragraph 3 sets forth an incorrect date of the filing of the petition. 3) Paragraph 6 sets forth an incorrect date of the grant. 4) Paragraphs 8 & 14 set forth an incorrect date of expiration of creditor claims. 5) Paragraph 9 needs to state the nature of each claim. CRC 7.403(3). 6) Paragraph 10 does not explain why no court approval of the claims has been obtained. Probate Code section 9252. 7) Paragraph 24(a) needs to include information required by CRC 7.250(a)(3)-(5). 8) Paragraph 24(d) does not state whether notice of proposed action was given. Probate Code section 10513. 9) Paragraph 28 does not state whether the former co-administrator is waiving or requesting a portion of the statutory compensation. Probate Code section 10805 & CRC 7.704(a). 10) Paragraphs 28 and 29 need to include all losses in calculating statutory compensation. Probate Code sections 10800(b) & 10810(b). 11) Paragraph 30 and Exhibits E and F need to include all information required by CRC 7.702. 12) Paragraph 33 requires a brief explanation as to what happened to the difference between the cash on hand and net proceeds from the sale. 13) Paragraph 48 identifies 13 individuals as the decedent's "heirs" under California laws of intestate succession, but only two individuals waived an account. 14) Exhibit D requires an explanation as to why postage should not be absorbed as part of statutory compensation. LRSF 14.91B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RAYMOND L. MAR REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondents Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One
rulings.Rulings: The parties are ordered to appear and update the Court on the status of the pending discovery reflected in these four motions. Prior to the hearing, the parties are directed to review the orders of this Court re discovery dated January 16, 2020 and February 28, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Responses to RFAs, Set One is granted in part and denied in part, and any confidential information produced will be subject to the January 16, 2020 Protective Order. No opposition was filed. The motion is denied in part as to the argument that previously-produced documents were not in conformity with current CCP section 2031.280; the current statute became applicable after responses to the RFAs were produced and after this motion was filed, and there is no showing that the documents that were produced in 2019 were not in compliance with the prior statute. The motion is also denied in part as to RFAs 14 and 15, as the disqualification claim has been decided and discovery on that issue is now not relevant. Respondent Mallette's Motion to Compel Further Responses to RFAs, Set One and Form Interrogatory 17.1 is granted in part and denied in part, insofar as any confidential information produced will be subject to the January 16, 2020 Protective Order. No opposition was filed. Petitioner is directed to respond in compliance with CCP section 2033.220. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Response to Special Interrogatories under CCP sections 2030.290 and 2030.300 is granted in part and denied in part. No opposition was filed. Petitioner is ordered to respond to special interrogatories Nos. 31 through 34, per CCP section 2030.290, sub. (b), and to provide further responses to each of the other special interrogatories. The Motion is denied in part, insofar as any confidential information produced will be subject to the January 16, 2020 Protective Order. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Response to Form Interrogatories is denied. The record does not show that Petitioner made objections that were without merit or too general in the Third Amended Response. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.310, subd. (a)(3).) Petitioner did make arguably meritless objections and unacceptable general objections in the September 30, 2019 response, but those objections were essentially abandoned after the court's January 16, 2020 order in a related discovery dispute, and Petitioner's Third Amended Response does not contain objections. The record shows no prejudice suffered by Respondents during the period from when the September 2019 objections were made to when they were effectively withdrawn prior to the hearing on this motion. There is no showing or argument that Petitioner's latest production in the Third Amended Response is incomplete. Counsels' communications regarding four responses in the Third Amended Complaint indicate that counsel is able to continue to meet and confer on these four outstanding responses. CCP section 2030.290 is inapplicable, because there were timely responses to the form interrogatories. All requests for sanctions are denied.

No opposition was filed. Petitioner is directed to respond in compliance with CCP section 2033.220. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Response to Special Interrogatories under CCP sections 2030.290 and 2030.300 is granted in part and denied in part. No opposition was filed. Petitioner is ordered to respond to special interrogatories Nos. 31 through 34, per CCP section 2030.290, sub. (b), and to provide further responses to each of the other special interrogatories. The Motion is denied in part, insofar as any confidential information produced will be subject to the January 16, 2020 Protective Order. Respondents' Motion to Compel Further Response to Form Interrogatories is denied. The record does not show that Petitioner made objections that were without merit or too general in the Third Amended Response. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 2031.310, subd. (a)(3).) Petitioner did make arguably meritless objections and unacceptable general objections in the September 30, 2019 response, but those objections were essentially abandoned after the court's January 16, 2020 order in a related discovery dispute, and Petitioner's Third Amended Response does not contain objections. The record shows no prejudice suffered by Respondents during the period from when the September 2019 objections were made to when they were effectively withdrawn prior to the hearing on this motion. There is no showing or argument that Petitioner's latest production in the Third Amended Response is incomplete. Counsels' communications regarding four responses in the Third Amended Complaint indicate that counsel is able to continue to meet and confer on these four outstanding responses. CCP section 2030.290 is inapplicable, because there were timely responses to the form interrogatories. All requests for sanctions are denied.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARRY JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/2020 (no earlier date available). A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) Is the beneficiary 25 years of age or older? 2) The costs of $936 must be itemized. 3) The account period must be stated. Probate Code section 1061(a). The period must start with the date of death. LRSF 14.59. 4) All allegations required by Probate Code section 1064(a) must be included. 5) A schedule of liabilities must be filed. Probate Code section 1063(g). 6) The opening balance and wire fee listed in the bank statement must be included in account schedules. Probate Code section 1062. 7) Schedule 1 does not show any interest on the bank deposit. 8) The bank statement shows funds exceeding the FDIC limit. 9) An allegation regarding outstanding bills due to a third party for preparing an account must be included. LRSF 14.67(L)(7)(a)(iii).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LERON DOYLE MCGOWAN JR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required pursuant to LRSF 14.3(A)(5). Court is to determine whether lost will should be admitted to probate. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 6th Amended General Order which took effect on 07/21/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/21/20. No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] No proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] No proof of service or proof of publication. The lodged will is a photocopy; an amended petition must be filed to clearly indicate that the will is lost. [LRSF 14.29.] Evidence will be required to overcome the presumption of revocation. [Probate Code Section 6124.] Notice of the amended petition will be required to be given and published. [CRC 7.53(a).] A non-resident personal representative will be required to post bond. [LRSF 14.37.]
